children.label: penetration testing tool
children.description: The specialised ICT tools which test security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information such as Metasploit, Burp suite and Webinspect.

children.label: Metasploit
children.description: The framework Metasploit is a penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information. The tool is based on the concept of 'exploit' which implies executing code on the target machine this way taking advantage of the bugs and vulnerabilities of the target machine.

children.label: THC Hydra
children.description: The package THC Hydra is a parallelized login cracker which tests security weaknesses of the systems' protocols for potentially unauthorised access to system information. The main features include network logon cracker and passwords reading and printing.

children.label: Parrot Security OS
children.description: The operating system Parrot Security is a Linux distribution which performs penetration cloud testing, analysing security weaknesses for potentially unauthorised access.

children.label: Aircrack (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The computer program Aircrack is a cracking program which recovers 802.11 WEP and WPA-PSK keys by making several network attacks such as FMS, KoreK and PTW attacks.

children.label: Cain and Abel (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The software tool Cain and Abel is a password recovery tool which tests the Microsoft Operating System for security weaknesses and potentially unauthorised access to system information. The tool decodes, decrypts and uncovers passwords by means such as brute-force and cryptanalysis attacks, network sniffing and protocols analysis.
altLabels: Cain and Abel penetration testing software
Cain and Abel penetration testing tool
Cain and Abel software for penetration testing
Cain and Abel password recovery tool

children.label: Nexpose
children.description: The computer program Nexpose is a specialised ICT tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information, developed by the software company Rapid7.

children.label: Nessus
children.description: The computer program Nessus is a specialised ICT tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information, developed by the software company Tenable Network Security.

children.label: BlackArch
children.description: The BlackArch Linux distribution is a penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information.

children.label: John The Ripper (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The tool John the Ripper is a password recovery tool which tests security weaknesses of the systems for potentially unauthorised access to system information. The key features of this tool are the strength-checking code and password hash code.

children.label: Kali Linux
children.description: The Kali Linux tool is a penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of the systems for potentially unauthorised access to system information by information gathering, vulnerability analysis and wireless and passwords attacks.

children.label: Wireshark
children.description: The Wireshark tool is a penetration testing tool which evaluates security weaknesses, analysing network protocols through deep protocol inspection, live capture, display filters, offline analysis, VoIP analysis, protocol decryption.

children.label: Samurai Web Testing Framework
children.description: The linux environment Samurai Web Testing Framework is a specialised penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of websites for potentially unauthorised access.

children.label: OWASP ZAP
children.description: The integrated testing tool OWASP Zed Attack Proxy (ZAP) is a specialised tool which tests web applications security weaknesses, replying on an automated scanner and a REST API.

children.label: Backbox (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The software BackBox is a Linux distribution which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information by information gathering, forensic, wireless and VoIP analysis, exploitation and reverse engineering.

children.label: WhiteHat Sentinel
children.description: The computer program WhiteHat Sentinel is a specialised ICT tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information, developed by the software company WhiteHat Security.

children.label: Maltego
children.description: The platform Maltego is a forensic application that uses data mining to deliver on overview of organisations' environment, testing security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access and demonstrates the complexity of infrastructure failures.

children.label: identify ICT security risks
children.description: Apply methods and techniques to identify potential security threats, security breaches and risk factors using ICT tools for surveying ICT systems, analysing risks, vulnerabilities and threats and evaluating contingency plans.
altLabels: identify ICT security risks and threats

children.label: perform ICT security testing
children.description: Execute types of security testing, such as network penetration testing, wireless testing, code reviews, wireless and/or firewall assessments in accordance with industry-accepted methods and protocols to identify and analyse potential vulnerabilities.
altLabels: prepare and apply security test plans

children.label: penetration testing tool
children.description: The specialised ICT tools which test security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information such as Metasploit, Burp suite and Webinspect.

children.label: Metasploit
children.description: The framework Metasploit is a penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information. The tool is based on the concept of 'exploit' which implies executing code on the target machine this way taking advantage of the bugs and vulnerabilities of the target machine.

children.label: Aircrack (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The computer program Aircrack is a cracking program which recovers 802.11 WEP and WPA-PSK keys by making several network attacks such as FMS, KoreK and PTW attacks.

children.label: Nessus
children.description: The computer program Nessus is a specialised ICT tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information, developed by the software company Tenable Network Security.

children.label: John The Ripper (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The tool John the Ripper is a password recovery tool which tests security weaknesses of the systems for potentially unauthorised access to system information. The key features of this tool are the strength-checking code and password hash code.

children.label: OWASP ZAP
children.description: The integrated testing tool OWASP Zed Attack Proxy (ZAP) is a specialised tool which tests web applications security weaknesses, replying on an automated scanner and a REST API.

children.label: Wireshark
children.description: The Wireshark tool is a penetration testing tool which evaluates security weaknesses, analysing network protocols through deep protocol inspection, live capture, display filters, offline analysis, VoIP analysis, protocol decryption.

children.label: Samurai Web Testing Framework
children.description: The linux environment Samurai Web Testing Framework is a specialised penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of websites for potentially unauthorised access.

children.label: THC Hydra
children.description: The package THC Hydra is a parallelized login cracker which tests security weaknesses of the systems' protocols for potentially unauthorised access to system information. The main features include network logon cracker and passwords reading and printing.

children.label: Maltego
children.description: The platform Maltego is a forensic application that uses data mining to deliver on overview of organisations' environment, testing security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access and demonstrates the complexity of infrastructure failures.

children.label: BlackArch
children.description: The BlackArch Linux distribution is a penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information.

children.label: Parrot Security OS
children.description: The operating system Parrot Security is a Linux distribution which performs penetration cloud testing, analysing security weaknesses for potentially unauthorised access.

children.label: WhiteHat Sentinel
children.description: The computer program WhiteHat Sentinel is a specialised ICT tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information, developed by the software company WhiteHat Security.

children.label: Cain and Abel (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The software tool Cain and Abel is a password recovery tool which tests the Microsoft Operating System for security weaknesses and potentially unauthorised access to system information. The tool decodes, decrypts and uncovers passwords by means such as brute-force and cryptanalysis attacks, network sniffing and protocols analysis.
altLabels: Cain and Abel penetration testing software
Cain and Abel penetration testing tool
Cain and Abel software for penetration testing
Cain and Abel password recovery tool

children.label: Nexpose
children.description: The computer program Nexpose is a specialised ICT tool which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information, developed by the software company Rapid7.

children.label: Kali Linux
children.description: The Kali Linux tool is a penetration testing tool which tests security weaknesses of the systems for potentially unauthorised access to system information by information gathering, vulnerability analysis and wireless and passwords attacks.

children.label: Backbox (penetration testing tool)
children.description: The software BackBox is a Linux distribution which tests security weaknesses of the system for potentially unauthorised access to system information by information gathering, forensic, wireless and VoIP analysis, exploitation and reverse engineering.
